proxy-checker.whatItIs: The Proxy Checker detects whether you are using a proxy or VPN and shows your apparent IP address and location. Verify your anonymity settings and ensure your proxy configuration is working correctly.

howToUse.name: How to Use the Proxy Checker

steps.name: Visit the tool page
steps.text: Open the Proxy Checker tool. Your connection information is detected automatically without any input required.

steps.name: View proxy status
steps.text: See whether you are connected through a proxy or VPN. Check the apparent IP address, location, and ISP shown to websites.

steps.name: Compare real vs apparent
steps.text: Compare your real IP with the apparent IP seen by websites. Verify that your proxy is masking your actual location.

steps.name: Test anonymity
steps.text: Check the anonymity level of your proxy connection. Transparent proxies reveal your real IP, while elite proxies provide maximum anonymity.

faqs.question: How does the proxy checker work?
faqs.answer: The tool analyzes HTTP headers, IP addresses, and connection properties to detect proxy and VPN usage. It compares your apparent IP with known proxy/VPN ranges.

faqs.question: What types of proxies does it detect?
faqs.answer: The tool detects HTTP proxies, HTTPS proxies, SOCKS proxies, VPN connections, and Tor exit nodes. It also identifies transparent, anonymous, and elite proxy types.

faqs.question: Can I test if my VPN is working?
faqs.answer: Yes, visit the tool while connected to your VPN. If the apparent IP and location differ from your real location, your VPN is masking your connection correctly.

faqs.question: Is the Proxy Checker free?
faqs.answer: Yes, checking proxy and VPN status is completely free with unlimited usage.

proxy-checker.seoDescription: Check proxy and VPN status online for free. Detect if you are using a proxy, verify anonymity level, and compare real vs apparent IP addresses.

ipv6-checker.whatItIs: The IPv6 Checker detects whether your internet connection and browser support IPv6, the next-generation internet protocol. Check IPv6 connectivity, assigned addresses, and protocol compatibility.

howToUse.name: How to Use the IPv6 Checker

steps.name: Visit the tool page
steps.text: Open the IPv6 Checker. Your connection is automatically analyzed for IPv6 support and connectivity.

steps.name: View IPv6 status
steps.text: See whether your connection supports IPv6. Check your IPv6 address, connectivity status, and protocol details.

steps.name: Compare IPv4 and IPv6
steps.text: Review both your IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. Check if you have native IPv6 or are using tunneling/translation technologies.

steps.name: Test connectivity
steps.text: Use the IPv6 connectivity test to verify that you can reach IPv6-only websites and services.

faqs.question: What is IPv6?
faqs.answer: IPv6 (Internet Protocol version 6) is the latest version of the Internet Protocol. It provides a much larger address space than IPv4 and is designed to replace IPv4 as the internet runs out of addresses.

faqs.question: Why does IPv6 matter?
faqs.answer: IPv6 enables more devices to connect to the internet and offers improvements in security and routing. Many new services and networks require IPv6 connectivity.

faqs.question: How can I get IPv6?
faqs.answer: Most modern ISPs support IPv6 automatically. If your ISP does not support IPv6, you can use tunnel brokers or transition technologies like 6to4 or Teredo.

faqs.question: Is the IPv6 Checker free?
faqs.answer: Yes, checking IPv6 support and connectivity is completely free with unlimited usage.

ipv6-checker.seoDescription: Check IPv6 support and connectivity online for free. Detect IPv6 address and protocol compatibility. Test your internet connection for IPv6 readiness.

isp-lookup.whatItIs: The ISP Lookup tool identifies your Internet Service Provider, IP address range, and connection details. Useful for troubleshooting network issues, verifying VPN connections, and understanding your internet connection.

howToUse.name: How to Use the ISP Lookup Tool

steps.name: Visit the tool page
steps.text: Open the ISP Lookup tool. Your ISP information is detected automatically from your IP address.

steps.name: View ISP details
steps.text: See your ISP name, IP address, AS number, organization, and approximate location. Check connection type and network details.

steps.name: Verify VPN status
steps.text: Check if your connection shows your real ISP or a VPN/proxy provider. Compare with your expected ISP.

steps.name: Use for troubleshooting
steps.text: Use the ISP information for network troubleshooting, geolocation verification, or understanding your connection details.

faqs.question: What information does ISP lookup show?
faqs.answer: The tool shows your ISP name, IP address, autonomous system number (ASN), organization name, connection type, and approximate geographic location.

faqs.question: How accurate is ISP detection?
faqs.answer: ISP detection is accurate based on IP address allocation databases. It shows the organization providing your internet connection, though resellers and MVNOs may show the parent network.

faqs.question: Can this detect VPNs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the tool shows if your apparent ISP is a known VPN or proxy provider. Compare the displayed ISP with your actual internet provider.

faqs.question: Is the ISP Lookup free?
faqs.answer: Yes, looking up ISP information is completely free with unlimited usage.

isp-lookup.seoDescription: Lookup ISP information online for free. Identify your Internet Service Provider, IP address, and connection details. Verify VPN and proxy connections.
